heading: Supervision of very large banks, savings associations, and credit unions
label: 5515 Supervision of very large banks, savings associations, and credit unions

children.text: an insured depository institution with total assets of more than $10,000,000,000 and any affiliate thereof; or
an insured credit union with total assets of more than $10,000,000,000 and any affiliate thereof.

children.label: A
children.text: assessing compliance with the requirements of Federal consumer financial laws;

children.label: B
children.text: obtaining information about the activities subject to such laws and the associated compliance systems or procedures of such persons; and

children.label: C
children.text: detecting and assessing associated risks to consumers and to markets for consumer financial products and services.

children.heading: Coordination
children.label: 2 Coordination
children.text: To minimize regulatory burden, the Bureau shall coordinate its supervisory activities with the supervisory activities conducted by prudential regulators and the State bank regulatory authorities, including consultation regarding their respective schedules for examining such persons described in subsection (a) and requirements regarding reports to be submitted by such persons.

children.label: A
children.text: reports pertaining to a person described in subsection (a) that have been provided or required to have been provided to a Federal or State agency; and

children.label: B
children.text: information that has been reported publicly.

children.heading: Preservation of authority
children.label: 4 Preservation of authority
children.text: Nothing in this title may be construed as limiting the authority of the Director to require reports from a person described in subsection (a), as permitted under paragraph (1), regarding information owned or under the control of such person, regardless of whether such information is maintained, stored, or processed by another person.1See References in Text note below.1

children.heading: Reports of tax law noncompliance
children.label: 5 Reports of tax law noncompliance
children.text: The Bureau shall provide the Commissioner of Internal Revenue with any report of examination or related information identifying possible tax law noncompliance.

children.heading: The Bureau to have primary enforcement authority
children.label: 1 The Bureau to have primary enforcement authority
children.text: To the extent that the Bureau and another Federal agency are authorized to enforce a Federal consumer financial law, the Bureau shall have primary authority to enforce that Federal consumer financial law with respect to any person described in subsection (a).

children.heading: Referral
children.label: 2 Referral
children.text: Any Federal agency, other than the Federal Trade Commission, that is authorized to enforce a Federal consumer financial law may recommend, in writing, to the Bureau that the Bureau initiate an enforcement proceeding with respect to a person described in subsection (a), as the Bureau is authorized to do by that Federal consumer financial law.

children.heading: Backup enforcement authority of other Federal agency
children.label: 3 Backup enforcement authority of other Federal agency
children.text: If the Bureau does not, before the end of the 120-day period beginning on the date on which the Bureau receives a recommendation under paragraph (2), initiate an enforcement proceeding, the other agency referred to in paragraph (2) may initiate an enforcement proceeding, including performing follow up supervisory and support functions incidental thereto, to assure compliance with such proceeding.

children.heading: Service providers
children.label: d Service providers
children.text: A service provider to a person described in subsection (a) shall be subject to the authority of the Bureau under this section, to the same extent as if the Bureau were an appropriate Federal banking agency under . In conducting any examination or requiring any report from a service provider subject to this subsection, the Bureau shall coordinate with the appropriate prudential regulator.section 1867(c) of this title

children.heading: Simultaneous and coordinated supervisory action
children.label: e Simultaneous and coordinated supervisory action
A
children.text: coordinate the scheduling of examinations of the insured depository institution, insured credit union, or other covered person described in subsection (a);

children.label: B
children.text: conduct simultaneous examinations of each insured depository institution or insured credit union, unless such institution requests examinations to be conducted separately;

children.label: C
children.text: share each draft report of examination with the other agency and permit the receiving agency a reasonable opportunity (which shall not be less than a period of 30 days after the date of receipt) to comment on the draft report before such report is made final; and

children.label: D
children.text: prior to issuing a final report of examination or taking supervisory action, take into consideration concerns, if any, raised in the comments made by the other agency.

children.heading: Coordination with State bank supervisors
children.label: 2 Coordination with State bank supervisors
children.text: The Bureau shall pursue arrangements and agreements with State bank supervisors to coordinate examinations, consistent with paragraph (1).

children.heading: Request
children.label: A Request
children.text: If the proposed supervisory determinations of the Bureau and a prudential regulator (in this section referred to collectively as the “agencies”) are conflicting, an insured depository institution, insured credit union, or other covered person described in subsection (a) may request the agencies to coordinate and present a joint statement of coordinated supervisory action.

children.heading: Joint statement
children.label: B Joint statement
children.text: The agencies shall provide a joint statement under subparagraph (A), not later than 30 days after the date of receipt of the request of the insured depository institution, credit union, or covered person described in subsection (a).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: If the agencies do not resolve the conflict or issue a joint statement required by subparagraph (B), or if either of the agencies takes or attempts to take any supervisory action relating to the request for the joint statement without the consent of the other agency, an insured depository institution, insured credit union, or other covered person described in subsection (a) may institute an appeal to a governing panel, as provided in this subsection, not later than 30 days after the expiration of the period during which a joint statement is required to be filed under paragraph (3)(B).

children.label: I
children.text: have not participated in the material supervisory determinations under appeal; and

children.label: II
children.text: do not directly or indirectly report to the person who participated materially in the supervisory determinations under appeal; and

children.label: ii
children.text: one individual representative, to be determined on a rotating basis, from among the Board of Governors, the Corporation, the National Credit Union Administration, and the Office of the Comptroller of the Currency, other than any agency involved in the subject dispute.

children.label: I
children.text: shall include in its appeal all the facts and legal arguments pertaining to the matter; and

children.label: II
children.text: may, through counsel, employees, or representatives, appear before the governing panel in person or by telephone; and

children.label: I
children.text: may request the insured depository institution, insured credit union, or other covered person described in subsection (a), the Bureau, or the prudential regulator to produce additional information relevant to the appeal; and

children.label: II
children.text: by a majority vote of its members, shall provide a final determination, in writing, not later than 30 days after the date of filing of an informationally complete appeal, or such longer period as the panel and the insured depository institution, insured credit union, or other covered person described in subsection (a) may jointly agree.

children.heading: Public availability of determinations
children.label: D Public availability of determinations
children.text: A governing panel shall publish all information contained in a determination by the governing panel, with appropriate redactions of information that would be subject to an exemption from disclosure under .section 552 of title 5

children.heading: Prohibition against retaliation
children.label: E Prohibition against retaliation
children.text: The Bureau and the prudential regulators shall prescribe rules to provide safeguards from retaliation against the insured depository institution, insured credit union, or other covered person described in subsection (a) instituting an appeal under this paragraph, as well as their officers and employees.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: F Limitation
children.text: The process provided in this paragraph shall not apply to a determination by a prudential regulator to appoint a conservator or receiver for an insured depository institution or a liquidating agent for an insured credit union, as the case may be, or a decision to take action pursuant to section 1831 of this title or , as applicable.osection 1790a of this title

children.heading: Effect on other authority
children.label: G Effect on other authority
children.text: Nothing in this section shall modify or limit the authority of the Bureau to interpret, or take enforcement action under, any Federal consumer financial law, or the authority of a prudential regulator to interpret or take enforcement action under any other provision of Federal law for safety and soundness purposes.
